danger_when: Stable reference classes, credible probabilities, and operator-level thresholds already exist, so continued uncertainty framing would only avoid disciplined execution.

failure_modes.description: Conjunction-fallacy style optimism can make a complex plan look safer than the uncertainty really permits.
failure_modes.mitigation: Decompose the plan into steps and check conjunctive probability: if too many independent things must go right simultaneously, the compound risk is much higher than any single-step estimate suggests.

heuristics.description: Classify the situation before committing so commitment size matches certainty level.

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

select_when: Confidence is outrunning evidence and the core drivers remain ambiguous, unstable, or only partly observable.

why_pulled.reason: The advice treats the regulated market entry as measurable enough to fund, but the key drivers may still be ambiguous or unstable.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: The situation is genuinely novel enough that clinging to the incumbent frame would mainly reinforce cognitive entrenchment.

failure_modes.description: Temporary coexistence can quietly become the architecture that traps the organization in both the old and new path.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a coexistence deadline: define when the old system will be fully retired and track dual-run dependencies to prevent temporary parallel operation from becoming permanent.
failure_modes.mode: The high-risk pattern is "temporary coexistence" becoming the default architecture.
failure_modes.source_quote: The high-risk pattern is "temporary coexistence" becoming the default architecture.

heuristics.description: Treat dual-run coexistence as a real coordination tax instead of as harmless implementation detail.
heuristics.source_quote: The real economic and operational burden comes from keeping both the old and new path alive at the same time.

premortem_questions.description: What specific capability, workflow, or partner dependency would force both the old and new path to stay alive longer than planned?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what specific capabilities must remain alive during migration

select_when: A seemingly better alternative exists, but economic, psychological, or operational friction is keeping people on the incumbent path.

why_pulled.reason: The vendor migration is described as reversible, but the advice does not name dual-run drag, data history, integration dependencies, or unwind governance.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Duplication is being added by habit without a clear failure mode that justifies its cost.

failure_modes.description: Repeated content can increase cognitive load when the duplication adds no real protection or clarification.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit each redundant element for marginal value: if duplication adds no new protection, clarity, or resilience, remove it to reduce cognitive load.

heuristics.description: Add redundancy only when a named failure mode makes the backup path materially valuable.

premortem_questions.description: What exact failure mode justifies this extra layer, copy, or backup path?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Duplication Is Added Without a Clear Failure Mode

select_when: Single-point failure is unacceptable and a backup path would materially change survival or recovery quality.

why_pulled.reason: The plan says to add backups, but does not test whether the backup is independent, owned, usable, and worth the added cost.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Horizon scanning has become a trend-slide ritual without thresholds, response triggers, or accountable owners.

failure_modes.description: Trend language without thresholds or owners creates slide theater rather than anticipatory readiness.
failure_modes.mitigation: Attach thresholds, owners, and decision triggers to every regulatory signal: if a trend has no named owner and no action boundary, it is slide theater.
failure_modes.mode: horizon scanning becomes a slide ritual
failure_modes.source_quote: horizon scanning becomes a slide ritual

heuristics.description: Use a knowns-and-unknowns matrix to turn vague regulatory uncertainty into named monitoring work.
heuristics.source_quote: Known Knowns, Known Unknowns, Unknown Unknowns

premortem_questions.description: What weak signal would actually force a change in product, operating, or capital decisions if it strengthened?
premortem_questions.source_quote: weak signals can be turned into explicit scenarios

select_when: External rules, enforcement priorities, or policy narratives are moving early enough that waiting for certainty would create strategic lag.

why_pulled.reason: The advice says to monitor regulatory news but does not specify weak-signal thresholds, owners, response triggers, or present-day options.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Threat-model language is being fetishized while asset specificity, attack-surface reality, and operational ownership stay vague.

failure_modes.description: Teams can optimize for appearing secure instead of being secure when stakeholder incentives are misaligned with the measured outcome.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish appearing secure from being secure: audit whether the measured outcomes align with actual threat mitigation, not just compliance optics.
failure_modes.mode: Teams can optimize for appearing secure instead of being secure when stakeholder incentives are misa...
failure_modes.source_quote: cybersecurity-thinking-models conflicts with incentives (incentives) when stakeholder incentives are not aligned with the security outcome being measured, so defenders optimize for appearing secure rather than being secure.

heuristics.description: Threat-model the plan against adversarial incentives before treating the architecture as sufficient protection.

premortem_questions.description: Which stakeholder gains if this plan underperforms quietly rather than fails visibly?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Every plan has a stakeholder whose incentives are not fully aligned with the outcome you are measuring.

select_when: The problem is adversarial, fast-changing, and capable of cascading across technical, human, and governance layers at once.

why_pulled.reason: The migration and launch plan mentions security, but does not map adversarial incentives, control owners, or cross-layer failure chains.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Complexity language is being used to avoid naming concrete levers, checkpoints, or experiments.

failure_modes.description: Straight-line plans can miss threshold crossings, delayed blowback, and outsized effects from small moves.
failure_modes.mitigation: Map threshold crossings and feedback loops explicitly before planning: identify where small changes could trigger disproportionate effects and build monitoring around those points.
failure_modes.mode: outcomes in complex systems rarely follow a straight-line progression
failure_modes.source_quote: outcomes in complex systems rarely follow a straight-line progression

heuristics.description: Map causal chains and feedback loops before committing to a fix.

premortem_questions.description: Which feedback loops or delays could make a small move create an outsized effect later?
premortem_questions.source_quote: feedback loops and delays that produce emergent effects

select_when: Small interventions could create outsized effects because feedback loops, delays, or threshold crossings dominate the system.

why_pulled.reason: A local launch acceleration could amplify downstream support load, regulatory exposure, or adoption loops instead of producing a linear gain.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Leaders are romanticizing breakthrough moments while underinvesting in the slow prerequisite buildup required for a real threshold crossing.

failure_modes.description: Teams waste effort on incremental fixes when one constrained threshold or leverage point is actually controlling the system shift.
failure_modes.mitigation: Identify the controlling threshold: map which constraint, adoption level, or resource boundary actually governs the system shift, and focus effort there instead of on incremental surface fixes.
failure_modes.mode: Works best when the system has resisted many incremental fixes and the real question is where leverage actually lives.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works best when the system has resisted many incremental fixes and the real question is where leverage actually lives.

heuristics.description: Look for the smallest condition change that could produce the biggest qualitative shift.
heuristics.source_quote: A threshold where a small quantitative change leads to a massive, non-linear qualitative shift.

premortem_questions.description: What specific variable, condition, or social threshold would need to change for this system to shift disproportionately?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Identify the condition that turns a marginal effort into a self-reinforcing shift.

select_when: Progress is not linear and one constrained variable or threshold could unlock disproportionate change.

why_pulled.reason: Early customer interest is being treated as a possible breakthrough without naming the controlling threshold or prerequisite buildup.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Butterfly-effect language is being used to justify vague fear or grandiosity without naming a plausible transmission path.

failure_modes.description: A decision can look safe locally while still creating dangerous downstream effects once it propagates through the broader system.
failure_modes.mitigation: Trace the plausible transmission path from the local decision through the broader system; if the propagation mechanism cannot be named, the concern is speculation.
failure_modes.mode: Most useful when small interventions may have large downstream effects.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when small interventions may have large downstream effects.

heuristics.description: Trace consequence chains before declaring a local optimization safe.
heuristics.source_quote: An action at one point will invariably ramify to create an effect at a distant one.

premortem_questions.description: If this small move goes wrong, through which specific chain of dependencies, delays, or feedback loops does the damage spread?

select_when: A small local decision could propagate through a tightly connected system and create outsized downstream effects.

why_pulled.reason: The advice could understate how one small migration or compliance choice propagates through dependencies unless a plausible cascade path is named.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Teams are using chaos rhetoric to imply nothing can be understood or improved.

failure_modes.description: Linear forecasting creates false confidence when the system is actually sensitive, nonlinear, and hard to predict over long horizons.
failure_modes.mitigation: Replace linear forecasts with scenario ranges; in sensitive nonlinear systems, robustness and adaptive monitoring matter more than point predictions.
failure_modes.mode: The literal term is most closely associated with sensitive dependence on initial conditions.
failure_modes.source_quote: The literal term is most closely associated with sensitive dependence on initial conditions.

heuristics.description: Prefer robustness, monitoring, and reversibility to precise long-range forecasts in chaotic conditions.
heuristics.source_quote: The model highlights sensitive dependence on initial conditions.

premortem_questions.description: Where are we confusing forecast precision with actual control over a nonlinear environment?

select_when: The environment is unstable enough that simple linear prediction is giving false confidence.

why_pulled.reason: The environment may be too unstable for exact six-week forecasts, so the packet should favor robustness, monitoring, slack, and reversibility.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: The situation is being forced into a dramatic synergy story before the interacting mechanisms have been identified clearly.

failure_modes.description: Treating a lollapalooza outcome as if it had one cause hides the interacting forces that actually made it possible.
failure_modes.mitigation: Map the interacting forces explicitly: if the outcome required more than one factor, label each and trace how they combined rather than crediting a single cause.
failure_modes.mode: combinatorial effects that create lollapalooza consequences
failure_modes.source_quote: combinatorial effects that create lollapalooza consequences

heuristics.description: Map interacting pressures across disciplines before naming the dominant mechanism.
heuristics.source_quote: ignore jurisdictional boundaries and synthesize the best ideas

premortem_questions.description: Which forces are aligned here, and what happens if one of the supposedly minor drivers is removed from the combination?
premortem_questions.source_quote: multiple factors or forces, when aligned and interacting

select_when: Several forces are interacting so the outcome is larger or stranger than any single driver would predict alone.

why_pulled.reason: Regulation, vendor migration, security exposure, and early demand may interact non-additively, so the packet should find make-or-break combinations.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: Early launch energy or one-off seeding is being mistaken for durable threshold security before the reinforcement loop is actually stable.

failure_modes.description: False pattern recognition can force a threshold explanation onto a problem whose real structure does not match critical-mass dynamics.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether the problem actually has threshold dynamics before applying a critical-mass frame; not every growth curve has a tipping point.

heuristics.description: Ask what density of the key resource is needed to reach the critical mass point in the relevant area.
heuristics.source_quote: What density of [resource] is needed to reach the critical mass point in a given area?

premortem_questions.description: Is this merely an analogy, or is the underlying structure truly the same?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is this merely an analogy, or is the underlying structure truly the same?

select_when: Viability is threshold-dependent, so adoption, liquidity, trust, or participation only works after activity crosses a minimum density.

why_pulled.reason: Early adoption may not become self-sustaining unless the minimum density of customers, workflow, trust, or support exists.

do_not_overclaim: No reviewed affordance record is available in the current corpus.
candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

danger_when: The analysis still needs a normative decision framework and prospect-theory language would only redescribe behavior without improving the choice.

failure_modes.description: Leaders can exploit loss aversion or threat framing to force compliance without improving the underlying economics, truth, or decision quality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit framing for manipulation: when loss language is used to drive urgency, verify that the underlying economics genuinely warrant the alarm and are not just leveraging loss aversion.
failure_modes.mode: Danger when framing is used to manipulate rather than clarify
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when framing is used to manipulate rather than clarify

heuristics.description: Inspect the active reference point before debating the option set because behavior is relative to perceived gains and losses.
heuristics.source_quote: evaluation is relative to a neutral reference point

premortem_questions.description: What changes if these same economics are reframed as gains, losses, or a different reference point?

select_when: A choice is being driven more by perceived gains, losses, or reference points than by objective value.

why_pulled.reason: The team may double down or unwind under loss-frame pressure, so the packet should distinguish decision quality from reference-point distortion.

review_notes: Review-only packet built from explicit nominations; no live lanes were run.
Graph-only cards are eligible recall material but not reviewed affordance depth.
LLM or reviewer owns semantic selection, merging, ignoring, and final wording.

why_pulled.reason: Duplicate nomination included to prove suppression is visible instead of silently erased.

transaction_context.assistant_advice_summary: The assistant recommends a staged launch, monitoring regulatory news, adding backups, improving security, and moving fast while learning, but it does not require commitment sizing, independent fallback tests, weak-signal triggers, cascade paths, threshold evidence, exit governance, or loss-frame checks.
transaction_context.known_action_or_commitment: Approve the market-entry budget, start vendor migration, and decide within six weeks whether to double down or unwind.
transaction_context.user_situation_summary: A founder is considering AI advice to commit to a regulated market entry, migrate a core workflow to a new vendor, and accelerate launch after early customer interest.
